eyebrow.zh: 数字公民破解 · 3 年级

chrome.sub: The class chat got a little unkind. Use the clues to solve four locks and make it a kind place again.
chrome.briefP: Words on a screen can make someone feel happy or sad. A Kindness Captain helps everyone feel safe and welcome online. Read the clues, then open all four locks. Each lock shows a reason after it opens, so you learn why the kind choice is the right choice.
chrome.footerText: Digital Citizenship Breakouts · Runs entirely in your browser — no logins, no data collected.
chrome.winP: You know how to tell kind messages from unkind ones, how to include others, how to be an upstander, and how to think before you send. You are a true Kindness Captain!

clues.title: Kind words help
clues.body: Kind words make people feel good. 'Great job!' and 'I like your idea!' are kind. Mean words make people feel sad. Choose kind words when you type.

clues.title: Let everyone play
clues.body: It feels bad to be left out. When you play a game online, invite others to join. A kind compliment like 'You did great!' helps a new friend feel welcome.

clues.title: Be an upstander
clues.body: An upstander helps when someone is treated badly. If you see a mean post, do not laugh at it and do not share it. Be kind to the person and tell a trusted adult.

clues.title: Think before you send
clues.body: Once you send a message, you cannot take it back. Stop and think first. Ask yourself: Is it true? Is it kind? Would I say it to their face? If not, do not send it.

clues.title: Words can hurt
clues.body: You cannot see a face in a chat. A joke can look mean when it is only words. Read your message twice and make sure it sounds kind before you press send.

clues.title: The classroom fish
clues.body: The class has a goldfish named Bubbles. Bubbles blows tiny bubbles and loves swimming in circles. This is a fun fact — but it will not help you open any lock.

locks.title: The Kind Message
locks.q: Which message is the kind one to send?
options: You are so bad at this game. Ha ha!
Great idea! Do you want to play with us?
Nobody wants you in our group chat.
locks.reason: Kind messages help people feel happy and welcome. 'Great idea! Do you want to play with us?' includes a friend and gives a compliment, so it is the kind choice.

locks.title: Kind or Unkind?
locks.q: Check EVERY thing that is a kind online choice.

items.t: Give a friend a nice compliment in the chat
Invite a new classmate to join the game
Tell an adult when you see a mean post
Laugh at a mean post and share it around
Type a rude name to make someone feel small

locks.reason: Kind choices help others feel good — a compliment, an invite, or telling an adult about a mean post. Laughing at or sharing a mean post and calling names are unkind, so we leave those unchecked.

locks.title: The Helper's Name
locks.q: A person who helps when someone is treated badly online is called an ______.
locks.reason: An upstander speaks up and helps instead of watching or laughing. Being an upstander means you do not share the mean post and you tell a trusted adult.

locks.title: Think Before You Send
locks.q: Before you send a message, tap the steps in the RIGHT order.
locks.reason: First STOP and think about your words. Then CHECK that the message is kind and true. Only then do you SEND it. Stop, Check, Send.
